body.title: tests/test_routing.py::TestIsInUse::test_future_override_blocks failing
body.result: Patch target was igor.cognition.machine_manager but is_in_use lives in lab.utility_closet.machine_manager (the shim only re-exports). Swapped patch path to canonical module. Fixed 4 failing tests (3 TestIsInUse + 1 TestRoute); all 24 routing tests pass. Commit eebc7296.
body.description:
Discovered during T-cc-daily-chat-logs sprint on 2026-04-18. Pre-existing failure verified independently of that sprint's changes.

## Symptom
```
tests/test_routing.py:104: in test_future_override_blocks
    self.assertTrue(self._run(m))
E   AssertionError: False is not true
```

## Context
- Test suite otherwise clean: 2546 passed, 6 skipped
- test_routing.py is the only failure
- Only this one test in TestIsInUse fails (other routing tests unknown — suite halted on first fail with -x)

## First steps
- Read tests/test_routing.py around line 104 to understand what `_run(m)` checks
- Check git log on test_routing.py and the code under test for recent changes — might be a regression from a recent sprint
- Try running TestIsInUse class in full (without -x) to see the class-level pattern

## Scope
- Figure out whether test or code is wrong
- If test is stale, update it
- If code is wrong, fix it
- Keep change minimal — this is a targeted fix, not a routing refactor
